plan_metadata.description:
This is a new test plan. You can:

1. Record interactions using the VNC viewer (click 'Show VNC' and start recording)
2. Manually add steps one by one
3. Edit this description to describe your test workflow

Start by clicking 'Add Cell' below or begin recording your interactions!
plan_metadata.name: Customize python local debug

steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and Apps" option in the "New Project" popup within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension for Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Teams Agent with Data" dropdown option in the "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" selection menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Custom" option within the "Select an option to load your data" dropdown menu in the Teams Agent with Data extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Azure OpenAI" option in the "Service for Large Language Model (LLM)" dropdown menu within the Microsoft Visual Studio Code extension interface.
Type ${{secret:AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY}} into the Azure OpenAI Key input field in the Visual Studio Code pop-up dialog.
Press 'Enter' to submit the Azure OpenAI Key in the password input field within the Visual Studio Code authentication prompt.
Type ${{env:AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT}} into the "Azure OpenAI Endpoint" input field at the top of the VS Code window.
Press Enter to confirm the Azure OpenAI service endpoint in the input dropdown at the top of the VS Code window.
Type 'gpt-4.1' into the "Input Azure OpenAI deployment name now or set it later in the project" prompt at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.
Confirm the Azure OpenAI deployment name ("gpt-4.1") in the input prompt at the top of the VS Code window by pressing Enter.
Click the "Python" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown selection dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Default folder (/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects)" option in the "Workspace Folder" dropdown within Visual Studio Code to select the project root location.
Click the "README.md" file in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code to open its contents.
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code, while viewing the "Preview README.md" tab in a workspace named "VSCUSE_APP_CPGoU".
Type 'Python: Create Environment...' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input box at the top center of the interface.
Press Enter to select "Python: Create Environment..." from the VS Code command palette dropdown.
Click the "Venv - Creates a .venv virtual environment in the current workspace" option in the environment type dropdown of Visual Studio Code.
Type 'Python 3.12.12' into the Python interpreter selection input field at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Press Enter to select "Python 3.12.12 64-bit" from the interpreter dropdown in the "Select a Python installation to create the virtual environment" prompt within Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "src/requirements.txt" file entry in the "Select dependencies to install" dropdown dialog in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "OK" button in the dropdown menu confirming the selection of 'src/requirements.txt' in Visual Studio Code.
Click the blank area in the Visual Studio Code Explorer pane, below the file list and above the OUTLINE panel, to set focus to the Explorer panel or deselect any selected files.
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code while viewing the README.md preview.
Type "Notifications: Show Notifications" into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input box to trigger the command suggestion dropdown.
Press Enter to select the highlighted "Notifications: Show Notifications" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
@assertion notification "The following environment is selected" exist.
Click the "Type a message" input box at the bottom of the Microsoft Teams web app in the "vscuse_app_j8zNclocal" chat tab, indicated by the red crosshair.
Type "List Contoso history in table" into the message input box at the bottom of the vsuse_app_j8zNclocal tab in Microsoft Teams (web interface).
Click the "Send" button in the message input area to submit the text "List Contoso history in table" within the Microsoft Teams web app interface.
@assertion there's response message having text 1985
